per_turn_leaks.student_message: And when these episodes occur, what does your body do?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I start sweating really heavily and sometimes I feel a little off, like I might get sick. But I never actually throw up or anything.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: It usually starts with this tightness in my chest that feels like pressure. It's hard for me to describe, but it's more of a feeling than a sharp pain. Then I'll start to feel short of breath and my heart will be racing. Sometimes I get left arm tingling during the worst episodes.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: During an episode, do you notice anything besides the main symptom?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I don't think I mentioned that about the episodes getting really bad earlier. Let me clarify - occasionally when the tightness gets severe, I'll get this weird sensation in my left arm, like pins and needles or something. It doesn't happen every time, though.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, during these episodes, I do sweat a lot. That's really about all I notice.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now let's zoom out. Any swelling you've noticed anywhere on your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My shoes feel a bit tighter than usual lately and my ankles get swollen sometimes after long drives, but it's not too bad.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Let me ask about your medical history. What conditions have you been treated for?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I've had a few health issues in the past. I was diagnosed with hypertension about 8 years ago and type 2 diabetes about 5 years ago. And, oh yeah, my doctor told me I have high cholesterol too, but I stopped taking the medication for it after a year or so.

Also, I should mention that my father had a heart attack when he was around my age...

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My father had a heart attack when he was around my age, and unfortunately, it ended up being fatal. That's something that's always stuck with me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I've been feeling pretty stressed out about my symptoms lately, but other than that, I'd say I'm just feeling really tired and worn out from being on the road all the time as a truck driver. It's like I'm running on empty most of the time.

My health hasn't been great lately. I've had this nagging chest tightness for the past three days that's been keeping me up at night and making it hard to breathe when I'm doing physical activity.

I've been experiencing chest tightness for a few days now. It feels like pressure in my chest that gets worse when I exert myself or get anxious. Sometimes I feel short of breath and it's hard to catch my breath, but other than that, everything else seems normal.

During these episodes, I start sweating really heavily and sometimes I feel a little off, like I might get sick. But I never actually throw up or anything.

It usually starts with this tightness in my chest that feels like pressure. It's hard for me to describe, but it's more of a feeling than a sharp pain. Then I'll start to feel short of breath and my heart will be racing. Sometimes I get left arm tingling during the worst episodes.

I don't think I mentioned that about the episodes getting really bad earlier. Let me clarify - occasionally when the tightness gets severe, I'll get this weird sensation in my left arm, like pins and needles or something. It doesn't happen every time, though.

Yes, during these episodes, I do sweat a lot. That's really about all I notice.

Yeah, actually... I've noticed my stomach feels off during those episodes.

To be honest, besides the chest tightness and shortness of breath, I don't really notice anything else going on in my body during these episodes. It's like everything else just kinda shuts down until it passes.

No, I haven't experienced any fevers, chills, or night sweats.

My shoes feel a bit tighter than usual lately and my ankles get swollen sometimes after long drives, but it's not too bad.

I've been eating pretty much the same way I always do - lots of fast food on the road. As for my weight, I don't really think about it unless someone mentions it to me.

My father had a heart attack when he was around my age...

I'm currently taking Amlodipine 10mg daily for my hypertension, but to be honest, I don't always remember to take it on time because of my irregular schedule on the road. I do try to take it most days, but there are definitely times when I miss a dose or two.

As a truck driver, I'm on my feet for long hours, but it's not exactly exercise, if you know what I mean. I don't really have time to work out or do anything physical in my free time, so I'd say I'm pretty sedentary.

I eat a lot of fast food on the road because it's convenient and quick, but I know that's not good for me. My wife always tells me to eat healthier when I'm home, but it's hard to stick to it when you're driving all over the country.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: We've covered a lot. Is there anything we haven't discussed that you think is relevant?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've always been a little worried about my own health ever since my father had a heart attack at 52. It was pretty traumatic for us. And actually, I did have a routine checkup about a year ago when the doctor told me my cholesterol was high.
